questionHtml:
Consider the following reactions:

Fe2O3 (s) + CO (g) → 2FeO (s) + CO2 (g)       ΔHΘ = −3 kJ

Fe (s) + CO2 (g) → FeO (s) + CO (g)               ΔHΘ = +11 kJ

What is the ΔHΘ value, in kJ, for the following reaction?

Fe2O3 (s) + 3CO (g) → 2Fe (s) + 3CO2 (g)

 

A.   −25

B.   −14

C.   +8

D.   +19
